content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from ICM/RND curious agents) can be repurposed to prioritize which new experiences an active learning agent stores in its long-term memory, solving the 2024-2025 SOTA bottleneck of unstructured memory bloat that degrades active learning performance. Instead of only using uncertainty to select which data points to label next, the agent uses intrinsic curiosity reward as a secondary filter to decide which labeled experiences to retain permanently in working memory.
